question: Will shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz return to normal before April 27, 2026?
resolution_criteria: This question will resolve as Yes if, after March 16, 2026 and before April 27, 2026, 45 or more ships transit through the Strait of Hormuz during a single day, as reported by [IMF Portwatch](https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730).
background:
The [Strait of Hormuz](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Strait_of_Hormuz) is a narrow waterway that connects the Persian Gulf to the ocean, via the Gulf of Oman. The strait is bordered by Iran to the north and Oman and the United Arab Emirates to the south. Due to the amount of oil production in the Persian Gulf, 25% of the world's seaborne oil trade, as well as 20% of the world's liquefied natural gas, and a daily average of [60 ships](https://hormuzstraitmonitor.com/) has historically travelled through the strait. 

On February 28, 2026, Israel and the United States began [a sustained campaign of airstrikes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iran_war) against Iran, killing Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and attempting to topple the Iranian government. The strikes began a regional war, with Iran retaliating against Israel and the United States, as well as their assets in countries throughout the region. Shortly after the war began, Iran declared that ships would not be allowed to transit the strait, and on March 1, 2026, Iran [attacked several oil tankers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Strait_of_Hormuz_crisis).

A few days later, Iran clarified that the strait would be closed only to the U.S., Israel, and their western allies, and indeed Chinese, Turkish, and Saudi tankers have since been allowed to pass. However, the amount of [ship traffic](https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730) through the strait remains extremely low, and hundreds of ships remain [stranded](https://hormuzstraitmonitor.com/) in the Persian Gulf. As a result, global [oil prices](https://oilprice.com/oil-price-charts/) have skyrocketed.

U.S. President Donald Trump has made [conflicting statements](https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/mar/14/us-kharg-island-oil-export-hub) regarding how long the U.S. would continue to wage war, while the U.S has accused Iran of placing mines in the strait. Trump has [called on other nations](https://www.politico.com/news/2026/03/15/gulf-arab-states-intercept-new-missiles-and-drones-as-iran-threatens-to-widen-war-00829221) to send warships to the region in order to help reopen the strait but has [received muted reactions](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/15/world/middleeast/trump-strait-of-hormuz-iran-warships.html).
